steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: In a parallel circuit with two branches, what happens to the total current from the battery compared to a single-branch circuit?
options: The total current halves because it splits between branches
The total current stays the same regardless of the number of branches
The total current increases because there are more paths for charge to flow
The total current decreases because of more resistance
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Adding more parallel branches actually REDUCES the total resistance, so the total current from the battery INCREASES. Each branch provides an extra path for current to flow. The total current is the sum of the branch currents.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Adding parallel branches gives more paths for current. Total resistance decreases, so total current from the battery increases. The total current equals the sum of the branch currents.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.2.3 — Parallel circuits

steps.title: Parallel Circuits
steps.bodyHTML: In a parallel circuit, components are connected on separate branches. Three key rules: (1) The p.d. is the same across each branch. (2) The total current is the sum of the currents through each branch: Itotal = I1 + I2 + … (3) The total resistance is LESS than the smallest individual resistance: 1/Rtotal = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + …

relationships: V is the SAME across each parallel branch

commonErrors: Adding resistances directly in parallel (must use 1/R formula)
Thinking voltage splits in parallel (voltage is the SAME across branches)
Forgetting that total resistance in parallel is LESS than the smallest resistor

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Adjust the two parallel resistors. Notice the total resistance is always less than the smallest one.

aqa.questionText: Two resistors of 6.0 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in parallel to a 12 V supply. Calculate the total current drawn from the battery.

steps.label: Find total current

aqa.questionText: A 4.0 Ω and a 12 Ω resistor are connected in parallel to a 12 V battery. What is the total current from the battery?
aqa.markSchemeAward: 1 mark each branch current · 1 mark correct total + unit
aqa.commonMistake: Option C only calculates one branch (12/4 = 3.0 A) and forgets the other.

wjec.questionText: A 6.0 V supply powers two parallel branches drawing 2.0 A and 1.0 A. What is the total current?

equation.examTip: Total resistance in parallel is ALWAYS less than the smallest individual resistor. For two identical resistors, R_total = R / 2.
